title: Sweet Chicken Curry – ordinary curry, home curry
excerpt: Look, I like Indian, Malay and Thai curry. But I grew up with plain box-mix-sweet-apricot-jam curry. It takes me back to my childhood.

seo.title: Sweet Chicken Curry - ordinary curry, home curry - Marinda Kook

html: Look, I like Indian, Malay and Thai curry.  But I grew up with plain box-mix-sweet-apricot-jam curry.  It takes me back to my childhood.

recipe.title: Sweet Chicken Curry – ordinary curry, home curry
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 1 chicken stock cube or 10 ml powdered chicken stock
salt and pepper to taste

steps: I took a whole chicken and cut it into portions. It doesn't have to be done exactly right. Otherwise you can buy ready-cut chicken portions at your shop.
Cut your onion into rings and fry in oil until glassy. Now fry your finely chopped garlic together with the onion. Add your chicken and fry the chicken until slightly browned.
Peel the potatoes and cut them into quarters.  Add them to your chicken in the pot.
In a mixing bowl, dissolve your chicken stock in the warm water.
Now add all the rest of the ingredients to the mixing bowl with the chicken stock.
Pour it over the meat in the pot and let it simmer for about 60 minutes – it should now be falling off the bone and nicely cooked.
To thicken the curry sauce in your pot. Stir 10 ml cornflour into 80 ml cold water until it forms a paste. Stir it through your curry chicken and let it simmer for 10 minutes.

notes: Serve your chicken with rice and peas.  The colour combination is lovely.  I also enjoy a small dollop of chutney with my chicken.
